title: Quorum Queues, Raft Replication, Availability, and Data Safety · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A production review compares near-miss TypeScript messaging decisions for quorum queues, raft replication, availability, and data safety and requires the smallest defensible quorum queue safety and availability decision record with changed-case evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for quorum queue safety and availability decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose quorum queues for durable replicated data safety and reject them for high-churn temporary queues, lowest latency, or extreme backlogs better served by streams.
Derive tolerated failures and availability from odd replica count, online majority, leader election, placement, and maintenance sequence.
Combine persistent messages, publisher confirms, manual consumer acknowledgements, and duplicate-safe applications for quorum data safety.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Majority Loss Recovery
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz messaging team handles case 3178 in a high-volume telemetry route with one hot customer key. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to derive majority availability and combine it with publisher and consumer data-safety evidence; keep credentials and connection URLs out of learner output; then lower the byte or in-flight budget and preserve upstream rejection evidence. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Plan majority loss, delayed confirms, redelivery, operator checks, replica repair, and residual data-risk evidence without unsafe force recovery.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Plan majority loss, delayed confirms, redelivery, operator checks, replica repair, and residual data-risk evidence without unsafe force recovery.

hints: For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Majority Loss Recovery, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Majority Loss Recovery precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RabbitMQ and TypeScript Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Majority Loss Recovery, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A quiz messaging team handles case 3228 in a high-volume telemetry route with one hot customer key. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to derive majority availability and combine it with publisher and consumer data-safety evidence; keep credentials and connection URLs out of learner output; then raise a broker resource alarm and stop new publisher admission. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Plan majority loss, delayed confirms, redelivery, operator checks, replica repair, and residual data-risk evidence without unsafe force recovery.

For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Majority Loss Recovery, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Automatic reconnect immediately restores a quorum queue that permanently lost its majority.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using plan and majority plus specific case evidence.
